name: another-fastapi-jwt-auth

metadata.name: another-fastapi-jwt-auth
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation: # Another FastAPI JWT Auth [//]: # (![Tests](https://github.com/IndominusByte/fastapi-jwt-auth/workflows/Tests/badge.svg)) [//]: # ([![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/IndominusByte/fastapi-jwt-auth/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://coveralls.io/github/IndominusByte/fastapi-jwt-auth?branch=master)) [//]: # ([![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/fastapi-jwt-auth.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/fastapi-jwt-auth)) [//]: # ([![Downloads](https://static.pepy.tech/personalized-badge/fastapi-jwt-auth?period=total&units=international_system&left_color=grey&right_color=brightgreen&left_text=Downloads)](https://pepy.tech/project/fastapi-jwt-auth)) --- [//]: # (**Documentation**: https://indominusbyte.github.io/fastapi-jwt-auth) **Source Code**: https://github.com/delrey1/another-fastapi-jwt-auth/ --- ## Background This was forked from https://github.com/IndominusByte/fastapi-jwt-auth as it is no longer maintained. This release contains changes related to Pydantic >2 and PyJWT > 2. I used this on my own projects and will be updating it as required. PRs invited. ## Features FastAPI extension that provides JWT Auth support (secure, easy to use and lightweight), if you were familiar with flask-jwt-extended this extension suitable for you, cause this extension inspired by flask-jwt-extended 😀 - Access tokens and refresh tokens - Freshness Tokens - Revoking Tokens - Support for WebSocket authorization - Support for adding custom claims to JSON Web Tokens - Storing tokens in cookies and CSRF protection ## Installation The easiest way to start working with this extension with pip ```bash pip install another-fastapi-jwt-auth ``` ## License This project is licensed under the terms of the MIT license.

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.__init__
documentation:
Get jwt header from incoming request or get
request and response object if jwt in the cookie

```
param req

all incoming request

param res

response from endpoint
```

Get token from the headers

```
value from HeaderName
```

documentation:
---
param value: |-
  datetime with or without timezone, if don't contains timezone

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT._get_secret_key
documentation:
Get key with a different algorithm

```
param algorithm

algorithm for decode and encode token

param process

for indicating get key for encode or decode token

return

plain text or RSA depends on algorithm
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT._create_token
documentation:
Create token for access_token and refresh_token (utf-8)

```
param subject

Identifier for who this token is for example id or username from database.

param type_token

indicate token is access_token or refresh_token

param exp_time

Set the duration of the JWT

param fresh

Optional when token is access_token this param required

param algorithm

algorithm allowed to encode the token

param headers

valid dict for specifying additional headers in JWT header section

param issuer

expected issuer in the JWT

param audience

expected audience in the JWT

param user_claims

Custom claims to include in this token. This data must be dictionary

return

Encoded token
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT._has_token_in_denylist_callback
documentation: Return True if token denylist callback set

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT._check_token_is_revoked
documentation:
Ensure that AUTHJWT_DENYLIST_ENABLED is true and callback regulated, and then
call function denylist callback with passing decode JWT, if true
raise exception Token has been revoked

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT._get_expired_time
documentation:

```

return
```

```
return
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.set_access_cookies
documentation:
Configures the response to set access token in a cookie.
this will also set the CSRF double submit values in a separate cookie

```
param encoded_access_token

The encoded access token to set in the cookies

param response

The FastAPI response object to set the access cookies in

param max_age

The max age of the cookie value should be the number of seconds (integer)
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.set_refresh_cookies
documentation:
Configures the response to set refresh token in a cookie.
this will also set the CSRF double submit values in a separate cookie

```
param encoded_refresh_token

The encoded refresh token to set in the cookies

param response

The FastAPI response object to set the refresh cookies in

param max_age

The max age of the cookie value should be the number of seconds (integer)
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT._verify_and_get_jwt_optional_in_cookies
documentation:
Optionally check if cookies have a valid access token. if an access token present in
cookies, self.\_token will set. raises exception error when an access token is invalid
or doesn't match with CSRF token double submit

```
param request

for identity get cookies from HTTP or WebSocket

param csrf_token

the CSRF double submit token
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT._verify_and_get_jwt_in_cookies
documentation:
Check if cookies have a valid access or refresh token. if an token present in
cookies, self.\_token will set. raises exception error when an access or refresh token
is invalid or doesn't match with CSRF token double submit

```
param type_token

indicate token is access or refresh token

param request

for identity get cookies from HTTP or WebSocket

param csrf_token

the CSRF double submit token

param fresh

check freshness token if True
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.jwt_required
documentation:
Only access token can access this function

```
param auth_from

for identity get token from HTTP or WebSocket

param token

the encoded JWT, it's required if the protected endpoint use WebSocket to
authorization and get token from Query Url or Path

param websocket

an instance of WebSocket, it's required if protected endpoint use a cookie to authorization

param csrf_token

the CSRF double submit token. since WebSocket cannot add specifying additional headers
its must be passing csrf_token manually and can achieve by Query Url or Path
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.jwt_optional
documentation:
If an access token in present in the request you can get data from get_raw_jwt() or get_jwt_subject(),
If no access token is present in the request, this endpoint will still be called, but
get_raw_jwt() or get_jwt_subject() will return None

```
param auth_from

for identity get token from HTTP or WebSocket

param token

the encoded JWT, it's required if the protected endpoint use WebSocket to
authorization and get token from Query Url or Path

param websocket

an instance of WebSocket, it's required if protected endpoint use a cookie to authorization

param csrf_token

the CSRF double submit token. since WebSocket cannot add specifying additional headers
its must be passing csrf_token manually and can achieve by Query Url or Path
```

```
the encoded JWT, it's required if the protected endpoint use WebSocket to

param websocket

param csrf_token

the CSRF double submit token. since WebSocket cannot add specifying additional headers
its must be passing csrf_token manually and can achieve by Query Url or Path
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.fresh_jwt_required

```
param websocket
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.get_raw_jwt
documentation:
this will return the python dictionary which has all of the claims of the JWT that is accessing the endpoint.
If no JWT is currently present, return None instead

```
param encoded_token

The encoded JWT from parameter

return

claims of JWT
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.get_jti
documentation:
Returns the JTI (unique identifier) of an encoded JWT

```
param encoded_token

The encoded JWT from parameter

return

```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.get_jwt_subject
documentation:
this will return the subject of the JWT that is accessing this endpoint.
If no JWT is present, `None` is returned instead.

```
return

sub of JWT
```

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_jwt.AuthJWT.get_unverified_jwt_headers
documentation:
Returns the Headers of an encoded JWT without verifying the actual signature of JWT

```
param encoded_token
```

modules.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth
documentation: FastAPI extension that provides JWT Auth support (secure, easy to use and lightweight)

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.AuthJWTException
documentation: Base except which all fastapi_jwt_auth errors extend

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.InvalidHeaderError
documentation: An error getting jwt in header or jwt header information from a request

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.JWTDecodeError
documentation: An error decoding a JWT

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.CSRFError
documentation: An error with CSRF protection

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.MissingTokenError
documentation: Error raised when token not found

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.RevokedTokenError
documentation: Error raised when a revoked token attempt to access a protected endpoint

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.AccessTokenRequired
documentation:
Error raised when a valid, non-access JWT attempt to access an endpoint
protected by jwt_required, jwt_optional, fresh_jwt_required

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.RefreshTokenRequired
documentation:
Error raised when a valid, non-refresh JWT attempt to access an endpoint
protected by jwt_refresh_token_required

classes.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.exceptions.FreshTokenRequired
documentation:
Error raised when a valid, non-fresh JWT attempt to access an endpoint
protected by fresh_jwt_required

methods.name: another_fastapi_jwt_auth.auth_config.AuthConfig.token_in_denylist_loader
documentation:
This decorator sets the callback function that will be called when
a protected endpoint is accessed and will check if the JWT has been
been revoked. By default, this callback is not used.

*HINT*: The callback must be a function that takes decrypted_token argument,
args for object AuthJWT and this is not used, decrypted_token is decode
JWT (python dictionary) and returns *`True`* if the token has been deny,
or *`False`* otherwise.
